short_description: Review STIG change V-CHROME-004: Chrome extension installation must be restricted
description:
Change type: added
Impact: high_priority_review
Reason: A new high-severity control was added, so it should be triaged before lower-risk backlog work.
Changed fields: added
Vuln ID: V-CHROME-004
Rule ID: SV-CHROME-004r1_rule
Suggested owner: Endpoint/Windows Admin
Tags: GPO, Registry, Browser Security
Check summary: Review Chrome enterprise extension policy and confirm unapproved extension installation is blocked or allowlisted.
Fix summary: Configure Chrome ExtensionInstallBlocklist and ExtensionInstallAllowlist policies through GPO or registry.
Evidence needed: Screenshot or export of the relevant setting; GPO, registry, or Local Security Policy export; Date/time of validation; System or asset name; Reviewer notes

short_description: Review STIG change V-CHROME-003: Deprecated Chrome cleanup policy must be reviewed
description:
Change type: removed
Impact: review_recommended
Reason: The control was removed, so downstream tickets, evidence requests, or mappings may need cleanup.
Changed fields: removed
Vuln ID: V-CHROME-003
Rule ID: SV-CHROME-003r1_rule
Suggested owner: Security/GRC Analyst
Tags: Browser Security
Check summary: Review legacy Chrome cleanup policy settings.
Fix summary: Remove or disable legacy Chrome cleanup configuration.
Evidence needed: Screenshot or export of the relevant setting; Date/time of validation; System or asset name; Reviewer notes

short_description: Review STIG change V-CHROME-001: Chrome Safe Browsing enhanced protection must be enabled
description:
Change type: severity_increased
Impact: high_priority_review
Reason: The severity increased to high and the remediation text changed, so old tickets or implementation notes should not be reused without review.
Changed fields: title, severity, check_text, fix_text, references
Vuln ID: V-CHROME-001
Rule ID: SV-CHROME-001r2_rule
Suggested owner: Endpoint/Windows Admin
Tags: IAM, Endpoint Security, GPO, Registry, Browser Security
Check summary: Review Chrome enterprise policy in GPO or registry and export evidence showing SafeBrowsingProtectionLevel is set to enhanced protection.
Fix summary: Configure Chrome Safe Browsing enhanced protection through Group Policy or the registry policy path and document the applied baseline.
Evidence needed: Screenshot or export of the relevant setting; GPO, registry, or Local Security Policy export; Date/time of validation; System or asset name; Reviewer notes
u_stig_tags: IAM; Endpoint Security; GPO; Registry; Browser Security

short_description: Review STIG change V-CHROME-002: Chrome password manager must be disabled
description:

Changed fields: check_text
Vuln ID: V-CHROME-002
Rule ID: SV-CHROME-002r2_rule
Suggested owner: Endpoint/Windows Admin
Tags: Password Policy, GPO, Registry, Browser Security
Check summary: Review Chrome enterprise policy in GPO or registry and export evidence showing PasswordManagerEnabled is disabled for managed users.
